Title: Process a valid ADT^A01 admit message and create an inpatient encounter
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface (MLLP listener) is running and bound to a feed; sending application is registered in the message header config; a known patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send a well-formed ADT^A01 message over the MLLP channel | 2. Confirm the listener consumes the message | 3. Inspect the integration engine queue for processing status | 4. Open the patient record in the SUT
Expected_Result: Message is accepted, an inpatient (I) encounter is created and linked to patient PAT12345 with the correct ward/bed location, and a positive ACK (AA) is returned

Title: Process a valid ADT^A03 discharge and close the active encounter
Preconditions: Patient PAT12345 has an active inpatient encounter created via ADT^A01; MLLP listener is running
Test_Steps: 1. Send a well-formed ADT^A03 message referencing the active visit | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Open the patient encounter list
Expected_Result: The active encounter is closed with the supplied discharge date/time, patient status updates to discharged, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Process a valid ADT^A04 register for an outpatient and create the patient if absent
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; patient identity PAT99001 does not yet exist in the SUT
Test_Steps: 1. Send a well-formed ADT^A04 with full PID demographics | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Search for the new patient by identifier
Test_Data: MSH|...|ADT^A04|MSG0003|P|2.5; PID-3=PAT99001; PID-5=Doe^Jane; PID-7=19900115; PID-8=F; PV1-2=O
Expected_Result: A new patient record is created with mapped demographics, an outpatient (O) encounter is registered, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Process a valid ADT^A08 update and merge updated demographics
Preconditions: Patient PAT99001 exists with prior demographics; MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send a well-formed ADT^A08 with changed address and phone | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Open patient demographics
Expected_Result: Existing patient demographics are updated in place (no duplicate created), address and phone reflect new values, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Process a valid ORM^O01 new order message and create a pending order
Preconditions: Patient PAT12345 has an active encounter; order catalog includes the test code; MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send a well-formed ORM^O01 with ORC=NW (new) and OBR | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Open the patient orders list
Test_Data: MSH|...|ORM^O01|MSG0005|P|2.5; PID-3=PAT12345; ORC-1=NW; OBR-4=CBC^Complete Blood Count^L; ORC-2=ORD7001
Expected_Result: A new pending lab order is created for the patient with the correct test code and placer order number, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Process a valid ORU^R01 result and file numeric OBX observations
Preconditions: An order ORD7001 exists for patient PAT12345; MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send a well-formed ORU^R01 with OBR matching ORD7001 and OBX result segments | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Open the patient results viewer
Test_Data: MSH|...|ORU^R01|MSG0006|P|2.5; PID-3=PAT12345; OBR-2=ORD7001; OBX-2=NM; OBX-3=718-7^Hemoglobin^LN; OBX-5=13.5; OBX-6=g/dL; OBX-8=N
Expected_Result: Result is filed against the matching order, the numeric Hemoglobin value 13.5 g/dL displays with normal flag, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: File a critical (abnormal) OBX result and verify abnormal flag handling
Preconditions: An order exists for patient PAT12345; MLLP listener running; abnormal-flag rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with an OBX carrying a critical abnormal flag | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Open results viewer and any critical-result alert
Test_Data: MSH|...|ORU^R01|MSG0007|P|2.5; OBX-3=2160-0^Creatinine^LN; OBX-5=9.8; OBX-6=mg/dL; OBX-8=HH (critically high)
Expected_Result: Result is filed with a critically-high (HH) abnormal flag, surfaced visually as critical, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Reject a message with a missing required MSH-9 message type
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; message validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message whose MSH-9 field is empty | 2. Observe interface engine handling | 3. Check the returned acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Message is rejected as invalid, no encounter/patient change occurs, and a NAK (AR application reject or AE application error) is returned citing the missing message type

Title: Reject a message with missing required MSH-10 message control ID
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 with an empty MSH-10 field | 2. Observe engine handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Message is rejected, processing aborts, and a NAK (AR/AE) is returned indicating the missing message control ID

Title: Reject an ADT^A01 missing the required PID-3 patient identifier
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; PID-3 configured as required
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A01 with PID present but PID-3 empty | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Message is rejected because the patient identifier is missing, no patient is created/updated, and a NAK is returned

Title: Reject an ADT^A01 with an invalid PV1-2 patient class value
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; PV1-2 constrained to HL7 table 0004
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A01 with PV1-2 set to an out-of-table value | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Message is rejected or the segment flagged as invalid per HL7 table 0004, no encounter created, and a NAK/error ACK is returned

Title: Honor non-default encoding characters declared in MSH-2
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; engine reads MSH-2 dynamically
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message whose MSH-2 declares alternate encoding chars | 2. Confirm parser uses declared chars | 3. Verify a component-split field parses correctly
Test_Data: MSH|@#~\\&|...; PID-5 components separated by @ per declared field/component chars
Expected_Result: Parser uses the encoding characters from MSH-2 (not hardcoded defaults), the composite PID-5 name parses into components correctly, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Reject a message with a malformed MSH-2 encoding-characters field
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message where MSH-2 has fewer than the required encoding characters | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Test_Data: MSH|^~| (MSH-2 truncated, missing escape and subcomponent chars)
Expected_Result: Message is rejected as structurally invalid, no data is filed, and a NAK is returned identifying the malformed encoding characters

Title: Reject a message whose field separator is inconsistent with MSH-1
Preconditions: MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message that declares | as MSH-1 but uses a different separator mid-message | 2. Observe parse | 3. Check acknowledgment
Test_Data: MSH|^~\\&|...; later segment uses ; as field separator instead of |
Expected_Result: Parser fails or rejects the inconsistent segment, no partial bad data is filed, and a NAK/error ACK is returned

Title: Return a correctly structured AA acknowledgment for an accepted message
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; sending app awaits ACK
Test_Steps: 1. Send a valid ADT^A04 | 2. Capture the returned ACK message | 3. Parse MSA segment
Expected_Result: ACK message is returned with MSA-1=AA and MSA-2 echoing the original control ID MSG0015, structured per HL7 ACK schema

Title: Return AE (application error) when business processing fails on a structurally valid message
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; referenced order does not exist
Test_Steps: 1. Send a structurally valid ORU^R01 referencing a non-existent order | 2. Capture the ACK | 3. Parse MSA
Expected_Result: An ACK with MSA-1=AE is returned, MSA-3/ERR conveys the processing error, and the original control ID MSG0016 is echoed

Title: Return AR (application reject) for an unsupported message type
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; engine configured to only accept ADT/ORM/ORU
Test_Steps: 1. Send a syntactically valid but unsupported message type | 2. Capture ACK | 3. Parse MSA
Expected_Result: An ACK with MSA-1=AR is returned indicating the message type is not supported, and no data is filed

Title: Reject a message with an unrecognized segment ordering
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 where PID precedes MSH | 2. Observe parse | 3. Check acknowledgment
Test_Data: Segments out of order: PID|... then MSH|... (MSH not first)
Expected_Result: Message is rejected because MSH must be the first segment, no data is filed, and a NAK is returned

Title: Reject a truncated message cut off mid-segment
Preconditions: MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message that ends abruptly inside the PID segment | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment and queue
Test_Data: MSH|...|ADT^A01|MSG0019|P|2.5; PID|1||PAT123 (stream truncated, no segment terminator)
Expected_Result: Message is rejected/quarantined as incomplete, no partial patient data is filed, and a NAK or error is logged

Title: Reject a message containing non-printable control characters in a data field
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; input sanitization enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A08 with embedded null/control bytes in PID-5 | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check stored demographics and acknowledgment
Test_Data: PID-5 contains Doe^Jo\\x00hn with an embedded NUL byte
Expected_Result: Message is rejected or the control characters are safely stripped/escaped, no corrupted data is stored, and the event is logged

Title: Detect and suppress a duplicate message with a repeated MSH-10 control ID
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; duplicate detection enabled; message MSG0021 already processed successfully
Test_Steps: 1. Re-send the identical ADT^A01 with the same MSH-10 | 2. Observe handling | 3. Verify encounter count
Expected_Result: Duplicate is detected and not re-processed (no second encounter created), an idempotent AA ACK is returned, and the duplicate is logged

Title: Allow a legitimate retransmission flagged in MSH-15/MSH-16 accept/app ack mode
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; original delivery ACK was lost
Test_Steps: 1. Resend the original message per the accept-ack retransmission protocol | 2. Observe handling | 3. Verify single resulting record
Test_Data: MSH-15=AL; MSH-16=AL; same MSH-10 as a message whose ACK never arrived
Expected_Result: The system reconciles the retransmission to a single record (no duplicate), returns the expected ACK, and the sender's queue clears

Title: Map PID-3 with an assigning authority to the correct internal patient identity
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; identity cross-reference configured for assigning authority MRN-HOSP
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A08 with a qualified PID-3 | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify the resolved internal patient
Expected_Result: Identifier is matched to the correct internal patient using assigning authority and id type, demographics update on that single patient, and an AA ACK is returned
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; PHI/Privacy; Data Integrity; Standards-HL7

Title: Reject identity mapping when assigning authority is unknown
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; cross-reference has no entry for assigning authority FOREIGN-X
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A08 with PID-3 using an unknown assigning authority | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Mapping fails safely (no wrong-patient update, no silent new-patient creation unless policy allows), and a NAK/error ACK is returned for operator review
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; PHI/Privacy; Data Integrity; Standards-HL7

Title: Handle multiple repeating PID-3 identifiers (MRN plus SSN-type)
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; multiple identifier types accepted
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with two repeating PID-3 identifiers separated by the repetition char | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify both identifiers persist
Expected_Result: Both identifiers are stored and individually queryable, the correct primary identifier is used for matching, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Parse a composite PID-5 patient name into family/given/middle components
Preconditions: MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with a multi-component PID-5 | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify name components in the record
Expected_Result: Name components map correctly to family=Smith, given=Robert, middle=Allen, with suffix/prefix retained, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Validate PID-7 birthdate boundary values (min and max plausible dates)
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; date validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A04 with the earliest plausible DOB | 2. Send another with a far-future DOB | 3. Compare handling
Expected_Result: The valid historical DOB is accepted; the future DOB is rejected or flagged per business rule, with an appropriate ACK/NAK in each case

Title: Reject an invalid PID-8 administrative sex value outside HL7 table 0001
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; PID-8 constrained to table 0001
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with an out-of-table PID-8 | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Expected_Result: The out-of-table value is rejected or coerced to U per policy, the discrepancy is logged, and an appropriate ACK is returned

Title: Validate TS datetime fields with timezone offset are parsed correctly
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; engine timezone configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A01 with a fully qualified timestamp including offset | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify stored encounter time in local zone
Expected_Result: Timestamp is parsed with the -0500 offset and stored/displayed correctly normalized, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Reject an impossible datetime value in a TS field
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; date validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A03 with an invalid discharge datetime | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Test_Data: PV1-45 discharge datetime=20260732250000 (month/day/hour out of range)
Expected_Result: The impossible datetime is rejected, the discharge is not applied, and a NAK/error ACK is returned

Title: Reject an ORU^R01 missing the required OBR-4 universal service identifier
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; OBR-4 required
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with OBR present but OBR-4 empty | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Message is rejected because the service identifier is missing, the result is not filed, and a NAK is returned

Title: Validate OBX-2 value type matches OBX-5 content (NM vs numeric)
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; type checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an OBX declaring NM with a non-numeric value | 2. Send an OBX declaring NM with a valid number | 3. Compare handling
Test_Data: Case A: OBX-2=NM, OBX-5=POSITIVE; Case B: OBX-2=NM, OBX-5=13.5
Expected_Result: Case A is rejected/flagged for type mismatch; Case B is accepted and filed numerically; appropriate ACK is returned for each
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Clinical; Data Integrity; Standards-HL7

Title: File a coded (CE/CWE) OBX result with code system mapping
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; LOINC/SNOMED mapping configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with a coded OBX | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify the mapped concept in the record
Expected_Result: The coded result maps to the correct internal concept with the LOINC/local code system retained, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Handle multiple repeating OBX segments under a single OBR
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; multi-analyte panel order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with one OBR and several OBX result lines | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify all analytes filed
Test_Data: OBR-4=CMP^Comprehensive Metabolic Panel; OBX rows for Na, K, Cl, CO2 with values
Expected_Result: All OBX analytes are filed under the single panel/order, none dropped, displayed grouped, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Block ADT^A03 discharge when no active encounter exists (illegal transition)
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; patient PAT77001 has no open encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A03 for a patient with no admitted encounter | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check encounter list and acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Discharge is rejected as an illegal state transition (cannot discharge a non-admitted patient), no encounter is altered, and an AE/AR ACK is returned
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Clinical; Data Integrity; Standards-HL7

Title: Apply A01 then A08 then A03 in sequence and verify final discharged state
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; patient PAT77002 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A01 to admit | 2. Send ADT^A08 to update location | 3. Send ADT^A03 to discharge | 4. Review encounter history
Test_Data: A01 ward WARD-A; A08 ward WARD-B; A03 discharge=20260701200000
Expected_Result: Encounter transitions admitted -> location-updated -> discharged in order, final state is discharged at WARD-B, and each step returns AA

Title: Process out-of-order ADT messages using event timestamps to preserve correctness
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; sequencing by event time enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an A08 update timestamped AFTER an A03 discharge but deliver it BEFORE the A03 | 2. Then send the A03 | 3. Review final state
Test_Data: A08 event-time=20260701150000; A03 event-time=20260701140000 delivered after
Expected_Result: The system orders events by event time, the later A03 does not erroneously override the newer A08 if business rules require, and the final state is consistent (or the conflict is flagged)
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Data Integrity; Clinical; Standards-HL7

Title: Reject or hold a result (ORU) that arrives before its order (ORM)
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; order-before-result rule configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 referencing an order not yet received | 2. Observe handling | 3. Send the ORM, then re-check
Test_Data: ORU OBR-2=ORD8001 (no prior ORM); then ORM creating ORD8001
Expected_Result: The early result is held/queued or rejected with AE, and after the order arrives the result reconciles to the correct order (or requires reprocessing) without orphaning data
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Clinical; Data Integrity; Standards-HL7

Title: Verify MLLP framing with correct start/end block characters
Preconditions: MLLP listener running on configured port
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message wrapped in proper MLLP framing (VT ... FS CR) | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Send a message missing the trailing FS CR and compare
Test_Data: Frame: <VT>MSH|...<FS><CR> for valid; missing <FS><CR> for invalid
Expected_Result: The correctly framed message is consumed and ACKed; the improperly framed message is not consumed/times out, and the failure is logged

Title: Verify secure MLLP over TLS rejects an untrusted client certificate
Preconditions: MLLP-over-TLS listener configured with client-cert (mutual TLS) required
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to connect with an untrusted/expired client certificate | 2. Attempt to send a message | 3. Observe connection result
Test_Data: Client presents self-signed cert not in the trust store
Expected_Result: The TLS handshake is rejected, no message is accepted over the unauthenticated channel, and the rejection is logged for audit

Title: Sanitize HL7 escape sequences to prevent injection into downstream rendering
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; downstream viewer renders OBX text
Test_Steps: 1. Send an OBX whose text contains HL7 escape sequences and script-like content | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. View the result in the UI
Expected_Result: Escape sequences are decoded safely and any markup is neutralized/escaped on render so no injection executes, while legitimate text displays correctly

Title: Reject inbound messages from an unregistered sending application (MSH-3)
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; allowlist of sending applications configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 with an unregistered MSH-3 sending application | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment and audit log
Expected_Result: The message is rejected on authorization grounds, no data is filed, an AR ACK is returned, and the attempt is audit-logged

Title: Verify every accepted message generates an immutable audit entry
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; audit logging enabled; DB access for verification
Test_Steps: 1. Send a valid ADT^A04 | 2. Query the audit/integration log table | 3. Verify the audit fields
Expected_Result: An audit record is written capturing message control ID, type, sender, timestamp, and outcome; the record is immutable/append-only and ties to the patient touched

Title: Verify a failed message does not leave a partially-committed patient record
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; transactional processing; DB access
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A04 that passes structure but fails on a downstream constraint | 2. Observe rollback | 3. Query patient and encounter tables
Test_Data: ADT^A04 with a duplicate primary key collision on insert
Expected_Result: The transaction rolls back atomically leaving no partial patient/encounter rows, an AE ACK is returned, and the failure is logged

Title: Process a message at the maximum supported segment/field length boundary
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; max message size configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU^R01 with an OBX-5 text at the maximum allowed length | 2. Send one exceeding the limit | 3. Compare handling
Test_Data: OBX-5 length = max allowed (e.g., 65535 chars) vs max+1
Expected_Result: The at-limit message is accepted and filed without truncation; the over-limit message is rejected/handled gracefully (no crash), with appropriate ACK in each case

Title: Handle an empty optional repeating field versus a single null component
Preconditions: MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A08 with an explicitly null PID-11 address using HL7 null (\\"\"\") | 2. Send another omitting the field entirely | 3. Compare effects"
Test_Data: PID-11=\\"\"\" (HL7 explicit null) vs PID-11 absent"
Expected_Result: The explicit HL7 null clears the existing address (delete semantics) while the absent field leaves it unchanged, per HL7 null-handling rules; AA returned for both

Title: Process a message with UTF-8 / extended characters declared in MSH-18
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; MSH-18 character set honored
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 declaring UTF-8 with accented/CJK characters in PID-5 | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify stored name renders correctly
Test_Data: MSH-18=UNICODE UTF-8; PID-5=Munoz^Jose with accented chars and CJK sample
Expected_Result: Extended characters are decoded per the declared charset and stored/displayed without mojibake, and an AA ACK is returned

Title: Process both HL7 v2.3.1 and v2.5.1 messages on the same interface
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; multi-version parsing configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 with MSH-12=2.3.1 | 2. Send an ADT^A01 with MSH-12=2.5.1 | 3. Compare parsing of version-specific fields
Test_Data: Msg A MSH-12=2.3.1; Msg B MSH-12=2.5.1 (PID-3 CX structure differs)
Expected_Result: Both versions parse correctly against their respective schemas, version-specific field differences are honored, and both return AA

Title: Reject a message declaring an unsupported HL7 version in MSH-12
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; supported versions = 2.3.1 and 2.5.1
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 declaring an unsupported version | 2. Observe handling | 3. Check acknowledgment
Expected_Result: The message is rejected with an AR/AE indicating unsupported version, and no data is filed

Title: Exploratory session probing parser behavior with adversarial edge inputs
Preconditions: MLLP listener running in a test environment; tester may craft arbitrary messages
Test_Steps: 1. Probe empty segments, doubled separators, extra delimiters, and trailing whitespace | 2. Inject very long repetition lists | 3. Mix valid and invalid segments | 4. Record any crash, hang, silent drop, or data corruption
Test_Data: Variety: MSH|||||; duplicated || fields; 1000x repeated PID-3; mixed CR/LF terminators
Expected_Result: No crash, hang, memory blowup, or silent data corruption occurs; every input yields a deterministic accept-with-ACK or reject-with-NAK, and anomalies are logged for triage
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Data Integrity; Security; Standards-HL7

Title: Honor MSH-11 processing ID so test (T/D) messages do not affect production data
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; processing-ID routing configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 with MSH-11=T | 2. Send an identical one with MSH-11=P | 3. Verify routing/effect of each
Test_Data: Msg A MSH-11=T (test); Msg B MSH-11=P (production)
Expected_Result: The T message is routed to the test stream and does not alter production patient data; the P message updates production; routing is logged and ACKed appropriately
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Data Integrity; Regulatory/Compliance; Standards-HL7

Title: Process an ADT^A40 patient merge and reconcile duplicate identities
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; two patient records exist that represent the same person; merge supported
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A40 specifying surviving and merged identifiers (MRG segment) | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify identity consolidation
Expected_Result: The duplicate identity PAT-DUP is merged into PAT-SURVIVE, clinical data is retained under the surviving record, references update, and an AA ACK is returned
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; PHI/Privacy; Data Integrity; Standards-HL7

Title: Process an ADT^A11 cancel-admit and reverse the prior A01
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; patient PAT88001 was admitted via A01
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A11 referencing the prior admit | 2. Confirm consumption | 3. Verify the encounter state
Test_Data: PID-3=PAT88001; ADT^A11 cancel admit for the A01 encounter
Expected_Result: The erroneous admit is cancelled/voided (encounter removed or marked cancelled, not silently left open), state is consistent, and an AA ACK is returned
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Clinical; Data Integrity; Standards-HL7

Title: Verify the Resource Adapter Layer maps ADT^A04 to a FHIR Patient/Encounter on a FHIR backend
Preconditions: HAPI FHIR backend connected via the Resource Adapter Layer; mapping configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send a valid ADT^A04 through the adapter | 2. Confirm transformation | 3. Query the FHIR server for the created resources
Expected_Result: The adapter creates a FHIR Patient and an Encounter (class=AMB) with correctly mapped identifiers/demographics, and an AA ACK is returned to the HL7 sender

Title: Verify the omiiCARE platform adapter persists an ORU result via the Resource Adapter Layer
Preconditions: omiiCARE platform connected via the Resource Adapter Layer; order exists on the platform
Test_Steps: 1. Send a valid ORU^R01 through the adapter | 2. Confirm transformation to the platform result model | 3. Query the platform API for the result
Expected_Result: The adapter persists the result to the omiiCARE result model with the correct order linkage and value, and an AA ACK is returned to the HL7 sender

Title: Re-queue and retry a message after a transient downstream outage
Preconditions: MLLP listener running; downstream persistence layer temporarily unavailable; retry policy configured
Test_Steps: 1. Take the downstream store offline | 2. Send a valid ADT^A01 | 3. Restore the store | 4. Verify retry and final outcome
Test_Data: ADT^A01 MSG0056 while DB is down, then DB restored
Expected_Result: The message is held/queued (not lost) during the outage, automatically retried after recovery, processed exactly once, and finally ACKed

Title: Echo correct values in the returned MSH (sender/receiver swapped) of the ACK
Preconditions: MLLP listener running
Test_Steps: 1. Send a valid ADT^A04 | 2. Capture the ACK | 3. Inspect ACK MSH-3/4/5/6
Expected_Result: In the ACK, sending/receiving application and facility are correctly swapped (responder becomes sender), the ACK control ID is unique, and MSA references the original control ID
